The German Arbeitsagentur, commonly referred to as the Jobcenter in English, serves as a pivotal institution for job seekers and employees alike in Germany. Established under the Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur für Arbeit), the Jobcentre plays a crucial role in the country’s labor market, overseeing a wide range of services from job placement to unemployment benefits. The Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Germany's Federal Employment Agency, is in charge of this online portal, which is intended to assist people in locating and applying to a variety of educational and training opportunities around the nation. A Bildungsgutschein (BGS) is an educational voucher that you can obtain from the Agentur für Arbeit or Jobcenter when you are registered in Germany as unemployed. Here you will find information on what you should keep in mind when registering with the Agentur für Arbeit. The Agentur für Arbeit (Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Arbeitsagentur, BA or AfA) is responsible for unemployment benefits and the German labour market. The Agentur für Arbeit is not the same thing as the Jobcenter. Find your perfect job in Germany easily using Germany's major job board. Here you find all information. There’s no guarantee that the Agentur für Arbeit advisors will speak English, so if you don’t speak German, you may want to bring a German-speaker with you to your appointment. The Bundesagentur für Arbeit (BA for short, AA or Arbeitsagentur) (' Federal Employment Agency') is a German federal agency under the Federal Ministry for Labour and Social Affairs. Its headquarters are in Nuremberg. Its director is Andrea Nahles. [1] The BA manages job centres across Germany and administers unemployment benefits. The agency is further divided into Arbeitsagentur and Jobcenter. This guide will help you through the process of registering at the Jobcenter in Germany and clarify where to go for support.
